pr.title: Match and link ftlinkspecialn via hacky solution, cleanup inline names
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched and linked Link's neutral special source unit by changing ftLk_SpecialN.c from NonMatching to Matching, while also adjusting GALE01 symbol scopes/names so related data, especially sysdolphin baselib devcom bss/sbss, could match/link cleanly. The ftLink source changes are mostly matching-oriented refactors: renamed vague inline helpers, hoisted motion flag constants, split shared animation helpers into padded and non-padded variants, introduced duplicate zero Vec3 constants, and used explicit float literals/order-preserving structure. The decomp.dev bot reported +1616 matched code bytes, +6500 linked code bytes, +33024 matched data bytes, and 4 new matches including ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim, ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim, devcom .bss, and devcom .sbss.
what_changed: Updated configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftLink/ftLk_SpecialN.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Renamed inline helper unkCondition to isDrawback and replaced inlineA-style helper names with more meaningful helpers such as isDrawn, animate, animate_nopad, setupParts, updateParts, and doEndColl.
Hoisted ftLk SpecialN MotionFlags constants mf and coll_mf near the top of ftLk_SpecialN.c instead of leaving them lower in the file.

Added three static const Vec3 zero objects named zero, zero2, and zero3, replacing a prior single zero_c-style constant usage; symbols.txt maps these to local rodata at 0x803B7520, 0x803B752C, and 0x803B7538.

Split animation position update logic into animate with FORCE_PAD_STACK_16 and two FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 calls, and animate_nopad without those stack pads, then used them in start/loop animation callbacks.
Changed ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim and ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim control flow to call isDrawn, then animate, and transition to loop motion when no frames remain.

Kept Link arrow item behavior in helper form: isDrawn creates the arrow with it_802A83E0 at the left thumb bone when a command variable requests it, sets callbacks on success, and returns true on item creation failure path.
Refactored collision transition helpers for ground/air SpecialN states, using coll_mf and explicit 1.0f/0.0f float arguments to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

Adjusted many symbols.txt entries from global to local scope for generated/static data and callbacks, including HSD_DevCom callbacks, Link/ifStatus local string/data constants, ifTime bss objects, devComARQR/devComStatus, aramstate, dvdDC, aramDC, and related sbss entries.
Split HSD_DevCom_804C6330 bss from a single 0x8050 global object into a 0x10 global object plus local HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs at 0x804C6340 and local fileinfo at 0x804CE340.
Replaced some placeholder/generated symbol names with local compiler-like labels such as @23, @179, @338, @339, @24, and @267, and added section labels like ...rodata.0, ...data.0, and ...bss.0.
smart_moves: Used semantic helper names for previously opaque inlineA/unkCondition functions while preserving the inline-heavy shape needed for matching.
Introduced separate padded and non-padded animation helpers, acknowledging that identical-looking source logic may need different stack layouts at different call sites.
Used duplicate zero Vec3 constants rather than forcing one shared constant, which helped match the emitted rodata layout.
Moved symbol scopes to local where data/functions are compiler-local, reducing accidental global linkage pressure and enabling data-section matches.
Split a large devcom bss symbol into smaller accurately scoped objects, which the bot report shows contributed to devcom .bss reaching 100% matched.
Kept motion-state flag sets as named static const MotionFlags, making repeated transition calls clearer without losing exact flag composition.
Preserved matching-sensitive float behavior by using explicit 1.0f and 0.0f arguments and retaining a nearby '@todo Float order hack' in the enter routine.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action callbacks, cleanup can be safe if helper names improve readability but inline structure and call-site-specific stack behavior are preserved.
When one shared helper does not match all call sites, create near-duplicate helpers that differ only in padding or stack layout rather than over-generalizing.
Multiple identical static constants may be necessary to reproduce original rodata emission; do not assume deduplicating zero vectors is harmless.
A source unit becoming Matching may require symbol-map fixes outside that unit, especially local/global scope corrections and bss object splitting.
Compiler-like local labels such as @NNN and section labels like ...data.0 can be preferable to invented descriptive names when the object is truly local/generated and name evidence is weak.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for PR impact: here the important wins were two Link SpecialN animation functions and devcom bss/sbss data, not just the configure.py Matching flip.
naming_conventions: Replace placeholder inlineA0/inlineA1/inlineA2/inlineA3-style names with behavior-oriented helper names when behavior is visible from local code.
Use predicate-style names for boolean helpers: isDrawback and isDrawn.
Use short private helper names for repeated local behavior in a single move file: animate, animate_nopad, setupParts, updateParts, doEndColl.
Keep unknown move-state fields under existing struct naming when evidence is weak, e.g. mv.lk.specialn.x8, x14, unk_timer, and cmd_unk*_bool.
Use local @NNN names for anonymous static strings/floats when there is no stronger semantic name evidence.
For split bss arrays/buffers, suffixing with _bufs, as in HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs, documents the split without overclaiming a fully known type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: FORCE_PAD_STACK_16 and FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 were added inside animate to force a particular stack frame layout.
FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 and FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 were added in doEndColl to reproduce stack layout around local Vec3 temporaries and item operations.
Created animate_nopad for loop animation callbacks where the same Vec3 position math should not emit the padded stack shape.
Introduced three independent zero Vec3 constants to control rodata ordering and references.
Used explicit float literals in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, changing integer-looking 1/0 arguments to 1.0f/0.0f where needed.
Changed symbol scopes from global to local across data, sdata, sdata2, bss, sbss, and HSD_DevCom callback symbols to match actual linkage.
Added ...section.0 labels in symbols.txt at local object starts to support section/object boundary matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

The automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed positive impact: matched code 55.88% (+0.04%, +1616 bytes), linked code 32.28% (+0.17%, +6500 bytes), matched data 37.51% (+2.73%, +33024 bytes), linked data 32.07% (+52 bytes).
The bot listed 4 new matches: main/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom .bss, ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim, ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim, and main/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom .sbss.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that use duplicate zero Vec3 constants to match rodata layout.
Find ftLink or ftClink SpecialN work that reuses isDrawn, isDrawback, animate, or animate_nopad patterns.
Find PRs that split a large bss symbol into a header/control object plus local buffer object.
Find examples where FORCE_PAD_STACK was used only in one duplicate helper but not another identical-looking helper.
Check whether HSD_DevCom local/global symbol scope changes were followed by later devcom source/header typing improvements.
